No Poverty.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence directly links marketing management concepts or case studies to the eradication or reduction of poverty. 'Social responsibility' is too general.

Zero Hunger.reason: The case study '超商剩食大戰' explicitly addresses food waste, directly contributing to reducing food loss and ensuring access to food, aligning with Target 2.1 and 12.3.
evidence: 理念行銷、非營利組織行銷

Good Health and Well-being.reason: The '晶碩光學' case, a contact lens company, provides a very minimal, inferred link to health-related products, but does not address broader public health or well-being targets.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it directly provides tertiary education and skills for employment (Targets 4.3, 4.4). The core competence of understanding 'sustainability considerations' further aligns with education for sustainable development (Target 4.7).
evidence: Students will acquire and apply expertise in their respective fields.
understand and be able to identify the ethics, social responsibility, and sustainability considerations of business situations.

Gender Equality.reason: There is no explicit mention of gender equality or related issues within the course description, objectives, or weekly schedule. The initial inference was based on a misreading of the SDG targets reference.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus or case studies relates to clean water, sanitation, or water resource management.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus or case studies relates to affordable and clean energy, renewable energy, or energy efficiency.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course explicitly fosters 'entrepreneurship, creativity, and innovation' (Target 8.3). The inclusion of 'Sustainable Marketing' and 'CSR' directly supports decoupling economic growth from environmental degradation (Target 8.4).
evidence: 創新方法、創業態度與面對變革的能力

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course covers 'New Product Management' and fosters 'innovative approaches' (Target 9.5). The 'P&G Downy Enviro-Pak' case and '永續行銷' imply a focus on sustainable industrial processes and resource efficiency (Target 9.4).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: No explicit or strong inferred evidence directly links marketing management concepts or case studies to reducing income, social, economic, or political inequalities. 'Social responsibility' is too general.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus or case studies relates to sustainable urbanization, housing, transport, or public spaces.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: The course explicitly covers 'Sustainable Marketing' and 'CSR' (Target 12.6). Case studies like '超商剩食大戰' (food waste, Target 12.3) and 'P&G Downy Enviro-Pak' (sustainable packaging, Target 12.5) provide direct, strong evidence.

Climate Action.reason: The concepts of 'Sustainable Marketing' and the 'P&G Downy Enviro-Pak' case imply a focus on reducing environmental impact, which can be reasonably inferred to include climate change considerations (Target 13.2).

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus or case studies relates to marine ecosystems, ocean pollution, or sustainable fisheries.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or inferred evidence in the course syllabus or case studies relates to terrestrial ecosystems, biodiversity, or deforestation.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course emphasizes 'ethics' and 'honor' in business situations, including strict anti-plagiarism rules. This fosters integrity and accountability, which are foundational to strong institutions and reducing corruption (Targets 16.5, 16.6).
evidence: 理解與辨別商業環境中道德、社會責任與永續議題

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course covers 'non-profit organization marketing' and 'managing value networks and channels,' which inherently involve multi-stakeholder collaboration and partnerships, aligning with Target 17.17.
